decision_question: Which shard-007 row is currently safe as a default visible main-results exemplar?

what_must_not_be_claimed: Do not rewrite dossier depth on the same FCC cluster as if it created a second substantive shard-007 row.

decision_question: Which non-baseline row is currently the strongest governance-context or context-to-results bridge already in the package?
current_best_answer: U.S. institutional-response ladder / Pennsylvania-centered multistate bundle (PCPC-019-003; SRRM-019-002)

what_must_not_be_claimed: Do not relabel governance-response context as lifecycle closure, adjudicated event evidence, or a second default-visible result.

decision_question: Which row is the best next bounded hidden-to-visible upgrade candidate for a second shard-007 substantive row?

what_must_not_be_claimed: Do not promote from title gloss, translation alone, or unresolved local-news search friction.

decision_question: Which route should stay visible only as blocked-source or origin-side negative evidence until a new public surface appears?
why_this_is_the_current_best_answer: The route remains analytically important because it would deepen multi-authority reconstruction, and it is no longer pure title-only friction: archive-recovered official New Hampshire DOJ page text exists for multiple event pages even though all tested live canonical routes from this server still return 403.
what_must_not_be_claimed: Do not infer final chronology, exact allegations beyond the archived public text, adjudication, or stable live-host accessibility from archive recovery alone.

decision_question: Which route remains limitations-only because of rights/access and wording ambiguity rather than immediate promotion value?
